steps.title: Calculate the number of doubling periods
steps.content:
From 1647 to 1947, the time elapsed is $1947 - 1647 = 300$ years. Since the population doubles every 75 years, the number of doubling periods is:
$$\frac{300}{75} = 4$$
The population doubled 4 times in this span.

steps.title: Apply exponential doubling
steps.content:
When a quantity doubles $n$ times, it is multiplied by $2^n$. With 4 doublings:
$$P_0 \times 2^4 = 240,000$$
$$P_0 \times 16 = 240,000$$

steps.title: Solve for the initial population
steps.content:
Divide both sides by 16:
$$P_0 = \frac{240,000}{16} = 15,000$$
The population in 1647 was 15,000.
